![](data:image/png;base64,)

[Top 10 Deep Learning Frameworks in 2025 – 365 Data Science](https://365datascience.com/trending/deep-learning-frameworks/)

[https://codewave.com/insights/top-dl-frameworks/](https://codewave.com/insights/top-dl-frameworks/)

### Caffe

Older network. Still used for visual recognition applications, but you'd need to use a low level language to establish complex layer types

### Microsoft Cognitive Toolkit(CNTK)

### Theano

Built upon NumPy syntax. Stop dev in 2017

### Chainer

[Pytorch Vs Tensorflow Vs Keras: The Differences You Should Know](https://www.simplilearn.com/keras-vs-tensorflow-vs-pytorch-article)

PyTorch is better for testing and development, and smaller project, scientific research. Has a real time computing graph

Tensorflow is better for large scale deployment

Keras better for beginners and simplifies things

|   |   |   |   |
|---|---|---|---|
||Keras|PyTorch|TensorFlow|
|API Level|High|Low|High and Low|
|Architecture|Simple, concise, readable|Complex, less readable|Not easy to use|
|Datasets|Smaller datasets|Large datasets, high performance|Large datasets, high performance|
|Debugging|Simple network, so debugging is not often needed|Good debugging capabilities|Difficult to conduct debugging|
|Does It Have Trained Models?|Yes|Yes|Yes|
|Popularity|Most popular|Third most popular|Second most popular|
|Speed|Slow, low performance|Fast, high-performance|Fast, high-performance|
|Written In|Python|Lua|C++, CUDA, Python|

From <[https://www.simplilearn.com/keras-vs-tensorflow-vs-pytorch-article](https://www.simplilearn.com/keras-vs-tensorflow-vs-pytorch-article)>

Keras might not optimise as much as you could do with TensorFlow.

For more than one output category use softmax.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Hands-on Neural Networks With Keras: Design and Create Neural Networks Using Deep Learning and Artificial Intelligence Principles

Niloy Purkait, Purkait

From <[https://www.vlebooks.com/Product/Index/1239038](https://www.vlebooks.com/Product/Index/1239038)>

Linear regression, predicts a value using a straight line relationship

Y = a + mx

Polynomial regression, predicts a value but can use higher powers of x

Y = a + mx + m2x^2 +…. Mx^n

Multiple regression, Linear regression but with more than one independent variable

Y = a + mx + … Mz

Multivariate regression , regression where you predict multiple dependent variables at once

Logistic regression

Predicts the probability of a binary outcome using a logistic signoid function.

P = 1/(1+e^-i(a+mx))

Computational graph

You have tensors

You use backpropagation to update the weights.

[https://pytorch.org/blog/computational-graphs-constructed-in-pytorch/](https://pytorch.org/blog/computational-graphs-constructed-in-pytorch/)

[https://medium.com/analytics-vidhya/how-to-write-a-neural-network-in-tensorflow-from-scratch-without-using-keras-e056bb143d78](https://medium.com/analytics-vidhya/how-to-write-a-neural-network-in-tensorflow-from-scratch-without-using-keras-e056bb143d78)

Logits is the output of the neural network

Cross entropy for classificationF
